questions.prompt: What's the right notification-level default for a new assistant rule?

choices.text: 'Soon' — in-app inbox or one-time email. Users opt-up specific rules to push/SMS only when they care. Push-by-default leads to fatigue and mute; conservative defaults preserve attention and let users tune up the things that matter most.

questions.explanation: Push is precious. Default to less-urgent surfaces (in-app, daily digest); reserve push for things the user explicitly told you matter. The user's attention budget is finite; respect it.

questions.prompt: Your daily morning brief has 25 bullets across many categories. Open rate dropped after week 1. Best fix?

choices.text: Send the brief twice a day.

Trim to 6-10 most important items with clear actions; light categorization (2-3 categories max); skip days with nothing notable; add a 'see more' link for the long tail. Bullet walls don't get read; scannable digests do. Quality of selection > volume of items.
Use Opus to write fancier prose.

questions.explanation: A digest people actually read is 6-10 high-quality items, not 25 medium ones. The assistant's job is filtering, not summarizing-everything. Better selection compounds engagement; bullet walls compound fatigue.
